key_highlights: Global markets experienced a broad sell-off driven by inflation fears, rising bond yields, and geopolitical uncertainty, with the Dow briefly hitting 50,000 before reversing.
Bond vigilantes are back, with Wall Street pricing out rate cuts and eyeing hikes, as the global bond rout deepens and oil prices rise.
Tech sector faces a dual narrative: AI-driven gains (Cerebras IPO soars 90%, Nvidia extends winning streak) but a brutal job market bloodbath and rising yields pressure chipmakers.
Commodities slump across the board (copper, gold, metals) as inflation fears and a stronger dollar weigh, though hedge funds boost bullish copper wagers.
Stagflation risk rises to nearly 40% probability, with consumers feeling an affordability crunch and businesses facing spiking prices.
Key political developments: Fed Governor Miran resigns, Kevin Warsh tipped as new chair; Trump's trip yields few deals and no Iran progress; Venezuela starts $170B debt restructuring.

sectors.trend: Divergent performance: AI-related names (Cerebras, Nvidia, Marvell) rally on AI infrastructure demand, but broader tech sector is under pressure from rising yields, a job market bloodbath, and layoffs at companies like Kraken. Chipmakers are being dumped as yields rise.
sectors.implications: Investors should favor AI infrastructure plays (semiconductors, data centers) but avoid overvalued or non-AI tech. The tech job market contraction signals potential revenue headwinds for enterprise software and services.

sectors.trend: Oil rises on limited progress on Hormuz and Trump's China trip, but metals (copper, gold) slump on inflation fears and dollar strength. Natural gas rallies, benefiting Antero Resources. Copper sees hedge fund bullishness despite price dip.
sectors.implications: Energy remains a hedge against geopolitical risk, but commodity volatility is high. Gold may see long-term upside (Sprott's $10,000 target) but short-term pressure from rising real yields. Copper's long-term demand story (AI, electrification) intact.

sectors.trend: Bond vigilantes are back, with global bond selloff deepening. Credit markets are attracting yield-hungry investors as government debt sours. JPMorgan warns of spread widening, but CreditSights sees a lid on it. Berkshire builds a $2.6B stake in Delta, signaling value in airlines.
sectors.implications: Fixed-income investors should shift to credit for yield, but monitor spread widening risk. Banks may benefit from higher rates but face loan quality deterioration. Berkshire's move into airlines is a contrarian value signal.

sectors.trend: Consumers face an affordability crunch heading into summer, with $4.50 gas, rising business prices, and subscription fatigue. Retail sales remain solid but the 'shop 'til you drop' ETF is deteriorating. Car subscription services never took off, but flexibility is desired.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary names may face headwinds from inflation and reduced spending power. Discount grocers and value-oriented retailers (e.g., Canada's liquidation grocery stores) are booming. Subscription-based models may see churn.

sectors.trend: New York plans tax on cash home purchases over $1M. Zillow CEO prepares for next real estate revolution. Mortgage rates little changed despite inflation data. Blackstone's new data center REIT IPO may be too late. Data center demand from AI remains strong (Constellation Energy's $13B cash flow outlook).
sectors.implications: Real estate faces headwinds from high rates and potential tax changes, but data centers and AI infrastructure are bright spots. Residential real estate may see a shift in buyer behavior due to cash purchase taxes.

sectors.trend: Tempus AI (healthcare technology) is a bet for some funds. Hims & Hers bets big on GLP-1 drugs but investors want profits now. Boston Scientific fell on growth durability concerns. Aquestive Therapeutics had a Q1 earnings call.
sectors.implications: Healthcare AI and GLP-1 themes remain attractive but require patience on profitability. Medtech faces growth scrutiny. Investors should focus on companies with clear revenue visibility.

key_themes: Inflation fears and stagflation risk dominate, with bond yields surging and rate cut expectations evaporating.
AI infrastructure investment continues to drive gains in select tech names, but broader tech is under pressure.
Geopolitical uncertainty (Trump trip, Iran, Hormuz) adds to market volatility.
Commodity price divergence: oil up on supply risk, metals down on dollar strength.
Consumer affordability crunch and subscription fatigue signal potential demand slowdown.
Credit markets attract yield seekers as government bonds sell off.
Fed leadership transition (Miran resignation, Warsh nomination) adds policy uncertainty.
Crypto speculation shifts to micro-cap tokens, while Kraken cuts jobs and delays IPO.
Global bond rout is catching up with risk assets, leading to a broad sell-off.
Emerging markets suffer worst week since March on inflation fears.

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase allocation to inflation-protected securities (TIPS), commodities (energy, gold), and value stocks with pricing power. Reduce duration in fixed income.
Shorten bond duration, favor floating-rate notes, and consider credit for yield. Avoid long-duration equities (growth/tech) that are sensitive to rising discount rates.
Maintain energy exposure (oil, natural gas) as a hedge. Reduce exposure to markets directly affected by trade tensions (China, emerging markets).

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Tech Sector Layoffs / Job Market Contraction
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid overvalued tech names without AI exposure. Focus on companies with strong balance sheets and AI-driven revenue growth. Monitor consumer spending on tech services.
Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary, especially high-ticket items. Favor discount retailers, essential goods, and subscription models with high retention.
Reduce EM equity and bond exposure. Hedge currency risk via USD or gold. Focus on EM markets with strong commodity exports (e.g., Argentina's YPF project).
Prepare for potential hawkish shift under Warsh. Increase cash and short-term bonds. Monitor Fed communications closely.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase exposure to AI infrastructure (semiconductors, data centers, energy for AI)
opportunities.rationale: Cerebras IPO success, Nvidia's winning streak, and Constellation Energy's $13B cash flow outlook underscore strong demand. AI investment is secular, not cyclical.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy gold and silver on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: Billionaire Eric Sprott's 98% allocation and $10,000 gold target, plus inflation fears and geopolitical risk, support a long-term bullish case. Short-term weakness due to dollar strength is a buying opportunity.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in energy (oil & natural gas) for geopolitical hedge
opportunities.rationale: Oil rises on Hormuz tensions and limited progress from Trump's trip. Natural gas rally benefits producers. J.B. Hunt sees TL rates climbing 20% over 2 years, indicating transport demand.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider credit for yield as government bonds sell off
opportunities.rationale: Yield-hungry investors are moving to credit. Investment-grade and high-yield bonds offer attractive spreads. Berkshire's Delta stake signals value in beaten-down sectors.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Berkshire Hathaway as a defensive value play
opportunities.rationale: Berkshire's $2.6B Delta stake shows value orientation. Its cash pile and diversified holdings provide a buffer against market volatility. Warren Buffett's moves are often contrarian and prescient.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce duration in fixed income, increase cash and short-term Treasuries
defensive_moves.rationale: Global bond rout is deepening, with yields rising and rate cuts priced out. Short-term bonds minimize price risk and provide optionality to reinvest at higher rates.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge equity portfolios with put options or inverse ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Stocks posted worst day since March, and the sell-off may continue as bond yields rise and stagflation fears grow. Protect gains in overvalued sectors.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and non-AI tech
defensive_moves.rationale: Affordability crunch, subscription fatigue, and tech job market bloodbath signal weakening demand. Avoid companies with high exposure to consumer spending.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase allocation to defensive sectors (healthcare, utilities, staples)
defensive_moves.rationale: In a stagflation scenario, defensive sectors with pricing power and stable demand outperform. DTE Energy and Northrop Grumman are examples of resilient names.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Monitor and reduce EM exposure given inflation fears and dollar strength
defensive_moves.rationale: Emerging markets had their worst week since March. Dollar strength and rising yields are headwinds. Focus on commodity-rich EMs like Argentina (YPF) but with caution.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish with high volatility. Inflation fears, rising bond yields, and geopolitical uncertainty will continue to pressure equities, especially growth and tech. The bond rout may deepen, and the dollar will likely strengthen. Stagflation fears (40% probability) will keep risk assets under pressure. Energy and commodities may see further gains on supply disruptions. Credit markets will attract yield seekers but face spread widening risk.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously optimistic with selective opportunities. AI infrastructure investment is a secular trend that will drive growth in semiconductors, data centers, and energy. Inflation may moderate if the Fed under Warsh takes a credible hawkish stance. Gold and silver could rally to new highs. Emerging markets may recover once the dollar peaks. The consumer crunch may ease if gas prices stabilize. The tech job market may bottom out as AI creates new roles.
key_catalysts: Fed leadership transition (Warsh nomination) and any signals on rate path
Progress on Iran/Hormuz negotiations and oil supply dynamics
Cerebras IPO performance and follow-on AI company listings (SpaceX IPO filing as soon as Wednesday)
US retail sales and consumer confidence data for May/June
Inflation reports (CPI, PPI) for May and June
Corporate earnings for Q2 2026, especially from tech and consumer sectors
Geopolitical developments: Trump's trade deals, Venezuela debt restructuring, Turkey's debt default
Global bond market stabilization or further sell-off
China's EV expansion and trade tensions with the US
Crypto market trends: micro-cap token speculation and Kraken IPO delay
watch_list: US weekly jobless claims and JOLTS data
China PMI and industrial production data
Mortgage rates and housing market data
